problem_text_cn: 1079 光滑桌面上两个半径均为$R$、质量均为$m$的匀质圆盘$A、B$，$A$以平动速度$v$与静止的$B$相碰，接触时连心线与$v$夹角$45^\circ$。连心线方向为弹性碰撞，接触处切向摩擦因数为$\mu$。求碰后$A、B$的平动速度$\boldsymbol{v}_A、\boldsymbol{v}_B$和自转角速度$\boldsymbol{\omega}_A、\boldsymbol{\omega}_B$（按$x、y、z$轴分量表述）。
problem_text_en: 1079 Two homogeneous disks $A$ and $B$ with the same radius $R$ and mass $m$ on a smooth tabletop. Disk $A$ collides with the stationary disk $B$ at a translational velocity $v$, and the angle between the line connecting the centers and $v$ is $45^\circ$ at the moment of contact. The collision along the line connecting the centers is elastic, and the tangential friction coefficient at the contact point is $\mu$. Find the translational velocities $\boldsymbol{v}_A, \boldsymbol{v}_B$ and spin angular velocities $\boldsymbol{\omega}_A, \boldsymbol{\omega}_B$ of $A$ and $B$ after the collision (expressed in components along the $x, y, z$ axes).
img_cn: 图1079_1：两圆盘碰撞的速度分解与受力分析
img_en: Fig. 1079_1: Velocity decomposition and force analysis of the collision between two disks
problem_solution_cn: 【核心思路】分两种情况讨论，取决于摩擦作用时间是否与正碰作用时间相等：1. $\mu\leqslant1/6$时，摩擦力全程作用，弹性碰撞交换$x$方向速度，结合$y$方向动量定理和转动定理求解；2. $\mu>1/6$时，摩擦力先消失，接触点切向速度相等，联立方程求解最终速度与角速度。
problem_solution_en: 【Core Idea】Discuss in two cases depending on whether the friction action time is equal to the normal collision action time: 1. When $\mu\leqslant1/6$, friction acts throughout the process, elastic collision exchanges velocities in the $x$-direction, solve by combining the momentum theorem in the $y$-direction and the rotational equation of motion; 2. When $\mu>1/6$, friction disappears first, the tangential velocities of the contact points are equal, solve the final velocity and angular velocity by simultaneous equations.
problem_answer: 矢量表述：$\boldsymbol{v}_A=(0, v_{Ay}, 0)$，$\boldsymbol{v}_B=(\dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{2}v, v_{By}, 0)$，$\boldsymbol{\omega}_A=(0, 0, -\omega)$，$\boldsymbol{\omega}_B=(0, 0, -\omega)$；1. $\boldsymbol{\mu\leqslant1/6}$：$v_{Ay}=\dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{2}(1-\mu)v$，$v_{By}=\dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{2}\mu v$，$\omega=\dfrac{\sqrt{2}\mu v}{R}$；2. $\boldsymbol{\mu>1/6}$：$v_{Ay}=\dfrac{5\sqrt{2}}{12}v$，$v_{By}=\dfrac{\sqrt{2}}{12}v$，$\omega=\dfrac{\sqrt{2}v}{6R}$
